evidence_quotes.quote: protections against video game publishers permanently disabling games that have already been sold to customers.
online-only models that put purchased games at risk of permanent destruction when servers are shut down
Adding provisions for video game preservation to the Digital Fairness Act would address a legitimate consumer rights concern that affects millions of European gamers.

paragraphs.text: Dear European Commission, I am writing to respectfully suggest an additional consumer protection measure for the Digital Fairness Act that would complement the existing proposals addressing problematic digital practices. I would like to request consideration of protections against video game publishers permanently disabling games that have already been sold to customers. This practice has affected hundreds of titles over the past decade, leaving consumers unable to access products they have legitimately purchased. As a game-developer I have many concerns about current online-only games with no end-of-life preservation plan, I believe this issue deserves attention alongside the other digital fairness concerns being addressed. The gaming industry increasingly relies on online-only models that put purchased games at risk of permanent destruction when servers are shut down, even when offline functionality would be technically feasible. This creates a situation where consumers pay full price for products that may become completely inaccessible at the publisher's discretion. This issue has already garnered significant support, with over one million EU citizens signing the European Citizens' Initiative calling for game preservation protections. Adding provisions for video game preservation to the Digital Fairness Act would address a legitimate consumer rights concern that affects millions of European gamers. I believe protecting consumers' access to digital products they have purchased aligns well with the Act's broader goals of ensuring fair treatment in digital markets. Thank you for considering this suggestion and for your work on protecting consumers in the digital space. Respectfully, Oisín
